practicals.title: Map Work - Class IX History (French Revolution, Socialism in Europe, Nazism)
practicals.source_text: Outline political map of France/World. Locate/label/identify Bordeaux, Nantes, Paris, Marseille; Major countries of First World War and Second World War.

practicals.title: Map Work - Class IX Geography (India Size, Physical Features, Drainage)
practicals.source_text: Locate/label: India States/Capitals, Tropic of Cancer, Standard Meridian; Mountain Ranges, Peaks, Plateaus, Coastal Plains; Rivers and Lakes.
practicals.related_unit: Physical and Human Geography of India

practicals.title: Map Work - Class IX History (Nazism)
practicals.source_text: Outline Political Map of World. Locate/label Major countries of Second World War Axis and Allied Powers.

practicals.title: Map Work - Class IX Geography (Climate, Population)
practicals.source_text: Locate/label: Annual rainfall in India, Monsoon wind direction; Population density of all states.
practicals.related_unit: Physical and Human Geography of India

practicals.title: Map Work - Class X History (Nationalism)
practicals.source_text: Locate/label: Congress sessions (1920 Calcutta, Nagpur, Madras); Satyagraha movements (Kheda, Champaran, Ahmedabad); Jallianwala Bagh; Dandi March.

practicals.title: Map Work - Class X Geography (Resources)
practicals.source_text: Identify Major Soil Types. Locate: Salal, Bhakra Nangal, Tehri, Rana Pratap Sagar, Sardar Sarovar, Hirakund, Nagarjun Sagar, Tungabhadra.

practicals.title: Map Work - Class X Geography (Agriculture)
practicals.source_text: Identify: Major areas of Rice/Wheat; Largest/Major producer states of Sugarcane, Tea, Coffee, Rubber, Cotton and Jute.

practicals.title: Map Work - Class X Geography (Minerals)
practicals.source_text: Identify: Iron Ore Mines, Coal Mines, Oil Fields. Locate/label Power Plants Thermal and Nuclear.

practicals.title: Map Work - Class X Geography (Manufacturing)
practicals.source_text: Locating and labeling: Cotton textile Industries, Iron and Steel Plants, Software technology Parks.

practicals.title: Map Work - Class X Geography (Lifelines)
practicals.source_text: Locating/Labeling: Major Sea Ports, International Airports.

activities.title: Multiple Assessment Activities (Class IX)
activities.source_text: Quiz, debate, role play, viva-voce, group discussion, visual expression, interactive bulletin boards, gallery walks, exit cards, concept maps, peer assessment, self-assessment.

activities.title: Multiple Assessment Activities (Class X)
activities.source_text: Quiz, debate, role play, viva-voce, group discussion, visual expression, interactive bulletin boards, gallery walks, exit cards, concept maps, peer assessment, self-assessment.

projects.title: Class IX Interdisciplinary Project: Disaster Management
projects.source_text: Every student must undertake one project on Disaster Management. Objectives include creating awareness, preparing to face situations, and ensuring participation in disaster risk reduction plans.

projects.title: Class IX Interdisciplinary Project: Forest Society & Natural Vegetation
projects.source_text: Interdisciplinary project with Chapter 4 of History (Forest, Society and Colonialism) and Chapter 5 of Geography (Natural Vegetation and Wildlife). Compare forest situations in pre-colonial, colonial, post-colonial eras.
projects.related_unit: Livelihoods, Economies and Societies / Physical and Human Geography
projects.related_chapter: History Ch IV & Geography Ch V

projects.title: Class X Interdisciplinary Project: Globalisation and Lifelines of Economy
projects.source_text: Interdisciplinary project with History Chapter III (Making of a Global World), Geography Chapter 7 (Lifelines of National Economy), Economics Chapter 4 (Globalisation). Analyse implications, transportation as lifeline.
projects.related_chapter: History Ch III, Geo Ch VII, Eco Ch IV

projects.title: Class X Project Work: Consumer Rights/Social Issues/Sustainable Development
projects.source_text: Every student must undertake one project on Consumer Awareness OR Social Issues OR Sustainable Development. Objectives include gaining insight into themes and enhancing life skills.

marks_distribution: Class IX History: 20 Marks (18+2 map pointing)
Class IX Geography: 20 Marks (17+3 map pointing)
Class X History: 20 Marks (18+2 map pointing)
Class X Geography: 20 Marks (17+3 map pointing)
Internal Assessment Class IX/X: 20 Marks total (Periodic Test 5, Multiple Assessment 5, Subject Enrichment/Project Work 5, Portfolio 5)

Board Exam Question Paper Design: MCQs (1 Mark) - 20 marks; Long Answer (2-3 Marks) - 8+15=23 marks? No, text says 4x2=8 and 5x3=15. Case Study (4 Marks) - 12 marks. Long Answer (5 Marks) - 20 marks. Map Pointing - 5 marks.
excluded_or_non_assessed_content: Chapter 5 Pastoralists in the Modern World (Class IX History): Assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only
Chapter 1 The Story of Village Palampur (Class IX Economics): To be assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only
Chapter IV Natural Vegetation and Wildlife Map Pointing (Class IX Geography): Only map pointing to be evaluated in the annual examination
Chapter IV The Age of Industrialisation (Class X History): To be assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only
Subtopics 2-4.4 Making of a Global World: Evaluated in Board Examination, Subtopic 1-1.3 also included but specific evaluation noted for subtopics
teacher_notes: NEP 2020 emphasis on democratic outlook, liberty, equality, justice, diversity.
Interdisciplinary projects must be done at school only (April to September). Carryover of project to home strictly avoided.
Projects and models should be made from eco-friendly products without incurring too much expenditure.
Project records maintained for three months after result declaration.
Use maps available on Govt. of India website.
Art integration is required in projects (PowerPoint, Exhibition, Skit, etc.).
Competencies to be developed: Collaboration, Analytical skills, Evaluation, Synthesis, Creative solutions.
